x-enumeration-literals.name: Principal Rollover Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Automatically renews or reinvests the principal amount at the end of the term.

Comment:
Example:

A fixed deposit where the initial deposit amount is rolled over for another term.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Interest Rollover Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Automatically adds or reinvests the interest earned into the principal or continues earning interest in a new term.

Comment:
Example:

Interest earned on a certificate of deposit (CD) is compounded into the principal for the next term.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Full Rollover Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Both principal and accrued interest are rolled over into a new term.

Comment:
Example:

At maturity, a term deposit with $10,000 principal and $500 interest is reinvested as $10,500 for the next term.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Rollover Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Only a portion of principal and/or interest is rolled over; the remaining amount is withdrawn or allocated elsewhere.

Comment:
Example:

$5,000 principal is rolled over while $500 interest is paid out to the account holder.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Automatic Rollover Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The rollover occurs automatically at maturity without customer intervention, based on predefined rules.

Comment:
Example:

A fixed deposit automatically renews for the same tenure at prevailing rates.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Rollover Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rollover occurs only if certain conditions are met, such as minimum balance, interest rates, or customer consent.

Comment:
Example:

Term deposit rolls over only if the balance exceeds $1,000 and the interest rate meets a minimum threshold.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tiered Rollover Feature
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rollover terms or interest rates vary based on tiers, amounts, or product type.

Comment:
Example:

Higher principal amounts earn higher rollover rates upon reinvestment.
